  "text": "The Ceph repository implements the Cephx authentication protocol across multiple files in src/auth/ and src/mon/. The core protocol implementation is in src/auth/cephx/ which contains the main classes and structures.\n\nKey Classes and their Files:\n\n1. CephxClientHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxClientHandler.h) - Implements client-side Cephx authentication, extends AuthClientHandler, manages ticket requests and verification\n\n2. CephxServiceHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxServiceHandler.h) - Implements service-side authentication, extends AuthServiceHandler, handles incoming authentication requests and generates session keys\n\n3. CephxAuthorizeHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxAuthorizeHandler.h) - Implements the AuthAuthorizeHandler interface for verifying authorizers on secure connections\n\n4. KeyServer (src/auth/cephx/CephxKeyServer.h) - Central key management class handling ticket generation, key rotation, and service secret distribution. Contains key rotation logic for OSD, MDS, and MGR entities\n\n5. CephxSessionHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxSessionHandler.h) - Manages per-session cryptographic operations and message signing\n\nProtocol Message Structures (src/auth/cephx/CephxProtocol.h):\n\n- CephXAuthorizer - Authorizer structure with nonce, ticket, and session key\n- CephXTicketHandler - Handles individual service tickets and ticket validity\n- CephXTicketManager - Manages multiple service tickets and determines when new tickets are needed\n- CephXServiceTicket - Encrypted ticket containing session key and validity period\n- CephXServiceTicketInfo - Server-side ticket info with AuthTicket and session key\n- CephXAuthenticate - Client authentication request with challenges\n- CephXServerChallenge - Server's challenge value\n- CephXAuthorizeChallenge - Challenge structure for authorization phase\n- CephXAuthorizeReply - Server's response to authorization with nonce confirmation\n\nBase Authentication Classes (src/auth/):\n\n- AuthAuthorizer (Auth.h) - Abstract interface for authorizers across all auth protocols\n- AuthTicket (Auth.h) - Standard ticket structure with name, global_id, timestamps, and capabilities\n- RotatingSecrets (Auth.h) - Manages rotating key state for services with 3-key rotation (previous, current, next)\n- ExpiringCryptoKey (Auth.h) - Key with expiration timestamp\n- AuthAuthorizeHandler (AuthAuthorizeHandler.h) - Interface for authorization verification on incoming connections\n- AuthServiceHandler (AuthServiceHandler.h) - Base class for service-side authentication\n- AuthClientHandler (AuthClientHandler.h) - Base class for client-side authentication\n- AuthSessionHandler (AuthSessionHandler.h) - Interface for session message signing and verification\n- RotatingKeyRing (RotatingKeyRing.h) - Manages access to rotating secrets and entity keyrings\n\nMonitor-side Files (src/mon/):\n\n- AuthMonitor (AuthMonitor.h/cc) - Paxos-based monitor service managing authentication state and key distribution\n- MonClient (MonClient.h/cc) - Client connection handler for monitors including MonConnection class which manages per-connection authentication handshakes\n\nProtocol Message Types (src/messages/):\n\n- MAuth (MAuth.h) - Authentication request message with protocol type and payload\n- MAuthReply (MAuthReply.h) - Authentication reply with protocol, result code, global_id, and response data\n\nSupporting Files:\n\n- KeyRing (KeyRing.h/cc) - Persistent key storage for entities\n- Crypto (Crypto.h/cc) - Cryptographic primitives (AES encryption, HMAC-SHA256, etc.)\n- AuthRegistry - Protocol negotiation and selection\n\nKey Protocol Features Implemented:\n\n1. Key Rotation: RotatingSecrets maintains 3-version key rotation with previous, current, and next keys, with automatic expiration and renewal\n\n2. Ticket Generation: CephxKeyServer.build_session_auth_info() generates service tickets with session keys encrypted using principal keys\n\n3. Mutual Authentication: Challenge-response protocol between client and server using server challenges and client challenges\n\n4. Authorization: CephxAuthorizeHandler.verify_authorizer() validates signed authorizer messages on connections\n\n5. Capability System: Tickets include AuthCapsInfo encoding service-specific capabilities granted to the entity\n\n6. Session Establishment: Multi-phase handshake in CephxClientHandler and CephxServiceHandler including ticket request, session key derivation, and authorization"
}

{"blocks":[{"chunks":[{"content":"163: We build a cephx response header and call
164: ``cephx_build_service_ticket_reply()``.
165: 
166: ``cephx_build_service_ticket_reply()`` is in ``auth/cephx/CephxProtocol.cc``.  This 
167: routine will build up the response message.   Much of it copies data from its parameters to 
168: a message structure.  Part of that information (the session key and the validity period) 
169: gets encrypted with C's permanent key.  If the ``should_encrypt_ticket`` flag is set, 
170: encrypt it using the old ticket's key.  Otherwise, there was no old ticket key, so the 
171: new ticket is not encrypted.  (It is, of course, already encrypted with A's permanent key.)  
172: Presumably the point of this second encryption is to expose less material encrypted with 
173: permanent keys.
174: 
175: Then we call the key server's ``get_service_caps()`` routine on the entity name, with a 
176: flag ``CEPH_ENTITY_TYPE_MON``, and capabilities, which will be filled in by this routine.  
177: ","endLine":177,"startLine":163},{"content":"225: 
226: In this case, ``handle_request()`` will take the ``CEPHX_GET_PRINCIPAL_SESSION_KEY`` case. 
227: It will call ``cephx_verify_authorizer()`` in ``CephxProtocol.cc``.  Here, we will grab 
228: a bunch of data out of the input buffer, including the global and service IDs and the ticket 
229: for A.   The ticket contains a ``secret_id``, indicating which key is being used for it.     
230: If the secret ID pulled out of the ticket was -1, the ticket does not specify which secret 
231: key A should use.  In this case, A should use the key for the specific entity that C wants
232: to contact, rather than a rotating key shared by all server entities of the same type.
233: To get that key, A must consult the key repository to find the right key.   Otherwise, 
234: there's already a structure obtained from the key repository to hold the necessary secret.  
235: Server secrets rotate on a time expiration basis (key rotation is not covered in this
236: ... [chunk truncated]","endLine":243,"startLine":225},{"content":"330: 
331: Much of the security here falls apart if one of the permanent keys is compromised.  Compromise
332: of C's key means that the attacker can pose as C and obtain all of C's privileges, and can
333: eavesdrop on C's legitimate conversations.  He can also pretend to be A, but only in 
334: conversations with C.  Since it does not (by hypothesis) have keys for any services, he
335: cannot generate any new tickets for services, though it can replay old tickets and session
336: keys until S's permanent key is changed or the old tickets time out. 
337: 
338: Compromise of S's key means that the attacker can pose as S to anyone, and can eavesdrop on 
339: any user's conversation with S.  Unless some client's key is also compromised, the attacker
340: cannot generate new fake client tickets for S, since doing so requires it to authenticate
341: himself as A, using the client key it doesn't know.
342: ","endLine":342,"startLine":330}],"file":"doc/dev/cephx_protocol.rst","repo":"github.com/sg-evals/ceph--v19.2.1","type":"file","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/ceph--v19.2.1/-/blob/doc/dev/cephx_protocol.rst"}]}

28: enum class global_id_status_t {
29:   NONE,
30:   // fresh client (global_id == 0); waiting for CephXAuthenticate
31:   NEW_PENDING,
32:   // connected client; new enough to correctly reclaim global_id
33:   NEW_OK,
34:   // connected client; unknown whether it can reclaim global_id correctly
35:   NEW_NOT_EXPOSED,
36:   // reconnecting client (global_id != 0); waiting for CephXAuthenticate
37:   RECLAIM_PENDING,
38:   // reconnected client; correctly reclaimed global_id
39:   RECLAIM_OK,
40:   // reconnected client; did not properly prove prior global_id ownership
41:   RECLAIM_INSECURE
42: };

72:   uint64_t get_global_id() { return global_id; }
73:   global_id_status_t get_global_id_status() { return global_id_status; }

75: private:

77: 			       ceph::buffer::list *result,
78: 			       AuthCapsInfo *caps) = 0;

  "text": "The Ceph repository implements the Cephx authentication protocol across multiple files in src/auth/ and src/mon/. The core protocol implementation is in src/auth/cephx/ which contains the main classes and structures.\n\nKey Classes and their Files:\n\n1. CephxClientHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxClientHandler.h) - Implements client-side Cephx authentication, extends AuthClientHandler, manages ticket requests and verification\n\n2. CephxServiceHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxServiceHandler.h) - Implements service-side authentication, extends AuthServiceHandler, handles incoming authentication requests and generates session keys\n\n3. CephxAuthorizeHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxAuthorizeHandler.h) - Implements the AuthAuthorizeHandler interface for verifying authorizers on secure connections\n\n4. KeyServer (src/auth/cephx/CephxKeyServer.h) - Central key management class handling ticket generation, key rotation, and service secret distribution. Contains key rotation logic for OSD, MDS, and MGR entities\n\n5. CephxSessionHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxSessionHandler.h) - Manages per-session cryptographic operations and message signing\n\nProtocol Message Structures (src/auth/cephx/CephxProtocol.h):\n\n- CephXAuthorizer - Authorizer structure with nonce, ticket, and session key\n- CephXTicketHandler - Handles individual service tickets and ticket validity\n- CephXTicketManager - Manages multiple service tickets and determines when new tickets are needed\n- CephXServiceTicket - Encrypted ticket containing session key and validity period\n- CephXServiceTicketInfo - Server-side ticket info with AuthTicket and session key\n- CephXAuthenticate - Client authentication request with challenges\n- CephXServerChallenge - Server's challenge value\n- CephXAuthorizeChallenge - Challenge structure for authorization phase\n- CephXAuthorizeReply - Server's response to authorization with nonce confirmation\n\nBase Authentication Classes (src/auth/):\n\n- AuthAuthorizer (Auth.h) - Abstract interface for authorizers across all auth protocols\n- AuthTicket (Auth.h) - Standard ticket structure with name, global_id, timestamps, and capabilities\n- RotatingSecrets (Auth.h) - Manages rotating key state for services with 3-key rotation (previous, current, next)\n- ExpiringCryptoKey (Auth.h) - Key with expiration timestamp\n- AuthAuthorizeHandler (AuthAuthorizeHandler.h) - Interface for authorization verification on incoming connections\n- AuthServiceHandler (AuthServiceHandler.h) - Base class for service-side authentication\n- AuthClientHandler (AuthClientHandler.h) - Base class for client-side authentication\n- AuthSessionHandler (AuthSessionHandler.h) - Interface for session message signing and verification\n- RotatingKeyRing (RotatingKeyRing.h) - Manages access to rotating secrets and entity keyrings\n\nMonitor-side Files (src/mon/):\n\n- AuthMonitor (AuthMonitor.h/cc) - Paxos-based monitor service managing authentication state and key distribution\n- MonClient (MonClient.h/cc) - Client connection handler for monitors including MonConnection class which manages per-connection authentication handshakes\n\nProtocol Message Types (src/messages/):\n\n- MAuth (MAuth.h) - Authentication request message with protocol type and payload\n- MAuthReply (MAuthReply.h) - Authentication reply with protocol, result code, global_id, and response data\n\nSupporting Files:\n\n- KeyRing (KeyRing.h/cc) - Persistent key storage for entities\n- Crypto (Crypto.h/cc) - Cryptographic primitives (AES encryption, HMAC-SHA256, etc.)\n- AuthRegistry - Protocol negotiation and selection\n\nKey Protocol Features Implemented:\n\n1. Key Rotation: RotatingSecrets maintains 3-version key rotation with previous, current, and next keys, with automatic expiration and renewal\n\n2. Ticket Generation: CephxKeyServer.build_session_auth_info() generates service tickets with session keys encrypted using principal keys\n\n3. Mutual Authentication: Challenge-response protocol between client and server using server challenges and client challenges\n\n4. Authorization: CephxAuthorizeHandler.verify_authorizer() validates signed authorizer messages on connections\n\n5. Capability System: Tickets include AuthCapsInfo encoding service-specific capabilities granted to the entity\n\n6. Session Establishment: Multi-phase handshake in CephxClientHandler and CephxServiceHandler including ticket request, session key derivation, and authorization"
}

CephxAuthorizeHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxAuthorizeHandler.h) - Implements the AuthAuthorizeHandler interface for verifying authorizers on secure connections\n\n4. CephxSessionHandler (src/auth/cephx/CephxSessionHandler.h) - Manages per-session cryptographic operations and message signing\n\nProtocol Message Structures (src/auth/cephx/CephxProtocol.h):\n\n- CephXAuthorizer -
